runners.spotlight: Flopped when favourite for debut but took big step forward and put in good front-running shift when second of 11 at Salisbury (6f, soft) 16 days ago; big player if suited by this different trip-and-ground combination.
runners.comment: Good second at Salisbury (6f, soft) this month; big player if trip/ground suits here

runners.name: It'sbeenemotional
runners.spotlight: Did pretty well to finish fourth of ten on debut at Kempton (6f, AW) in May, given she didn't settle in the early stages, but she was below that form after again racing too freely at Lingfield (6f, AW); drops in trip for this turf debut but others look safer.
runners.comment: Showed promise but also a tendency to race too freely on her two 6f AW starts

runners.name: Nasserein
runners.spotlight: Failed to build upon fairly encouraging debut (5.7f, firm) when last of five (albeit not beaten far) at Ffos Las (5f, soft) last week but may do so now she's back on fast ground.
runners.comment: Showed some promise on debut and had a possible ground excuse on second start

runners.name: Kaiulani Spirit
runners.spotlight: Out of a useful 5f 2yo winner; 40-1 for debut at Carlisle (5f, good to soft) 16 days ago but displayed promise before fading into sixth; can build on that performance here and goes on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Out of a useful 5f 2yo winner and shaped with promise when sixth on Carlisle debut

runners.name: Articulate
runners.spotlight: £5,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; fourth foal; dam Listed-placed 7f winner (including 2yo; RPR 95), half-sister to winners Queen Of Deauville (5f 2yo) and Investigation (11.4f); the only newcomer in the line-up.
runners.comment: Out of a Listed-placed 2yo winner; cost £5,000 in April; only newcomer in the field

runners.name: Tallahassee Lassie
runners.spotlight: Respectable sixth of 12 at Ascot (5f, good) on debut in eary May and perhaps found 6f on soft ground too taxing when below that form at Goodwood a fortnight ago; remains a filly to be interested in.
runners.comment: Well held on soft ground last time but can still build upon her promising Ascot debut

runners.name: South West
runners.spotlight: Out of a 5f-winning half-sister to Breeders Cup Dirt Mile winner Furthest Land; unable to justify prominence in the betting when always behind in quite valuable Bath maiden (5.7f, good) three weeks ago but ought to have learnt from that experience.
runners.comment: Always behind when 5-1 for quite valuable Bath maiden; might have learnt from that outing

runners.name: Chianti
runners.spotlight: Well held when fifth of ten on debut at Windsor (5f, good to firm) 24 days ago but was 15-8 favourite for that and is presumably thought capable of much better; recently gelded.
runners.comment: Well held when fifth on debut but was 15-8 for that; presumably thought capable of better

runners.name: Fly Test
runners.spotlight: Well down the field on last month's debut at Newmarket (6f) and lost all chance at the start when dropped to 5f at Windsor a couple of weeks later (gelded soon afterwards); improvement required.
runners.comment: Well down the field on debut (6f) and lost all chance at the start last time (5f)

runners.name: Midnight's Dream
runners.spotlight: Five places and about 2l ahead of Mini Mac at Doncaster latest (1m, good), his best run for current yard; one win off 12lb higher and a 1l second (both mid-80s RPRs) from two previous visits to Leicester, and while both of those came over 1m he's still effective at 7f; visored last time but not today.
runners.comment: One win off 12lb higher and a second in two 1m starts here; still effective at 7f also.

runners.name: Moonhall Lass
runners.spotlight: Yarmouth and Salisbury 6f winner in the spring (good and good to firm) off at least 7lb lower, but diminishing returns in the cheekpieces since and visor is now reached for; she's still that bit more compelling at that sprint trip, regardless of posting one of her better 7f efforts latest.
runners.comment: Diminishing returns since two 6f turf wins in the spring off at least 7lb lower.

runners.name: Call Glory
runners.spotlight: Better on turf than a 1-21 record might suggest; two recent backward steps (never travelling here latest) but recorded a pair of near misses here before those, including over C&D off 2lb higher.
runners.comment: Went close here over 6f and C&D in the spring; two backwards steps since.

runners.name: Mini Mac
runners.spotlight: A repeat of the form which netted him a C&D second last month (good to firm) off today's mark would take him close; hard to blame his Doncaster flop since on the return to a 1m trip he has won over before.
runners.comment: Form of May's C&D second (good to firm) would take him close; flopped as favourite since.

runners.name: Dancing With Drums
runners.spotlight: Triple 6f scorer on Tapeta or Polytrack, and ought to have races in him on turf also judged on last month's Chepstow third (good to soft); this trip still seemed too far for now at Redcar latest, however.
runners.comment: Triple 6f scorer on AW; 7f still seemed too far for now at Redcar last time out.

runners.name: Bass Player
runners.spotlight: Not yet a year since he almost defied 8lb higher on quick turf (1m); mixed bag since, and hard to find any benefit from a third wind operation in the winter, but he's too well treated not to bear in mind at least.
runners.comment: Mixed bag since almost defying 8lb higher on quick turf last summer, but not without hope.

runners.name: Pisanello
runners.spotlight: Still capable of winning off 30lb higher exactly two years ago, but basically consistent at a lower level these days, and still well suited by a galloping turf track; this step up to 1m4f looks long overdue.
runners.comment: Back class in abundance; a step up to 1m4f seems overdue and this track will suit.

runners.name: Clipsham Noble
runners.spotlight: Last month's C&D success (good to firm) took his record in handicaps here to 3241; no surprise were he to reverse previous 1m2f course placings with Myna despite a 4lb re-raise, and Liam Wright can claim more here than in that apprentice race last time; player.
runners.comment: C&D winner off 4lb lower latest; can reverse previous 1m2f placings with Myna.

runners.name: Coverbridge
runners.spotlight: 14-race losing run now stretches back two years, with no trip this year (tried between 7f and 1m6f, including C&D) provoking a revival.
runners.comment: No trip this year (tried between 7f and 1m6f, including C&D) has yet provoked a revival.

runners.name: Duke Orsino
runners.spotlight: 0-13; kept on at Lingfield latest (good to firm) without improving for the step up to 1m2f, and he has further still to travel this time.
runners.comment: Kept on at Lingfield latest without improving for 1m2f; another new trip today.

runners.name: Ottoman
runners.spotlight: 1m2f winner here; back on track with this month's Bath third (1m3f, good) off 1lb higher; needs to back that up, having not been finishing his races off as well for a while previously, including behind Myna back here two starts ago.
runners.comment: 1m2f course winner; back to form at Bath last time (1m3f), but does need to back it up.

runners.name: Majestic Leo
runners.spotlight: 0-14 and, apart from one early bid for glory on Polytrack five starts ago, he is mostly struggling to break out of rear this term; opposed.
runners.comment: Apart from one early bid for glory, he's mostly struggling to break out of rear this term.

runners.name: Abila
runners.spotlight: Still early days for her on turf, but well held by Coverbridge and (especially) Clipsham Noble when a weakening 16l sixth over C&D latest (good to firm); 2lb drop doesn't tempt.
runners.comment: Well held by two of these when a weakening 16l sixth over C&D latest (good to firm).

runners.name: Pureis King
runners.spotlight: Immediate uplift for the switch to 1m2f classifieds, going close at Lingfield before an easy win at Ffos Las two days ago (good); likely stayer of further than that in this sphere as a former bumper winner, and mark looks no worse than fair; chance if this race doesn't come too soon.
runners.comment: Lingfield second and Ffos Las win (Tuesday) in 1m2f classifieds most recently; respected.

runners.name: Bibendum
runners.spotlight: Scored off today's mark over 5f on Tapeta in November and is also suited by fast turf but returned from four-month break with disappointing 6f run 19 days ago and needs to have taken significant benefit from that outing.
runners.comment: AW winner off this mark in the autumn but returned from break with low-key run this month

runners.name: Startling
runners.spotlight: Made low-key seasonal/stable debut over 6f last month but placed twice over 5f since, more recently when second on AW a week ago; back in action before 2lb rise takes effect; respected.
runners.comment: Close second on AW a week ago; back in action before 2lb rise takes effect

runners.name: Bang On The Bell
runners.spotlight: Has a mixed record this season and is not the force of old but ran a big race from the front when clear second to a dual subsequent winner at Nottingham (5f, good to soft) last month and will be fine on the faster ground here; could be hard to catch if in similar form again.
runners.comment: Inconsistent, but ran a big race behind a dual subsequent winner on his latest outing

runners.name: Dunnington Lad
runners.spotlight: Out of form this season (four runs) and remains without a win since summer 2024; can't be recommended from out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Out of form this season (four runs) and remains without a win since summer 2024

runners.name: Zooter
runners.spotlight: Neck ahead of Fille Imbassee on identical terms when third over C&D last month (good to firm); appeal undiminished by Ffos Las blowout since, when not taking to soft ground; 2lb badly in.
runners.comment: Just holds Fille Imbassee on last month's C&D clash; soft ground against him since.

runners.name: Fille Imbassee
runners.spotlight: Still finding her level on turf, but only a neck behind Zooter when fourth in a C&D 0-70 latest (good to firm), the new trip no issue; bit more needed despite slight ease in grade; trainer took this last year.
runners.comment: Neck behind Zooter when fourth in a C&D 0-70 last time (good to firm); bit more needed.

runners.name: Pacific Glory
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and mostly in rear on all three 7f novice/maiden starts; more positive signs late on at Catterick last time, however, and the seeming need for longer trip such as this is partly backed up by pedigree.
runners.comment: Most positive signs to date at Catterick latest (7f); longer trip a plus; may do better.

races.race_name: Dr T B Connors Memorial Handicap

runners.name: Pentonville
runners.spotlight: Much improved gelding who won recent handicaps at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) and Brighton (1m, good to firm) with quite a bit left in the tank; has a 12lb penalty to shoulder but still commands respect.
runners.comment: Much improved gelding who has recently gained two comfortable wins; commands respect
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Napolian
runners.spotlight: Made low-key seasonal/handicap debut when midfield at Thirsk (6f, good) three weeks ago and is now 0-5 as a sprinter; might have needed that recent outing and this new trip could plausibly unlock improvement.
runners.comment: 0-5 as a sprinter but this new trip could plausibly unlock improvement
